name: Wildflower Cottage Quilt
description: A quaint countryside cottage is partially hidden by the freshly laundered linens drying on the line aside a charming wildflower garden. Enter through the arched trellis of the garden gate covered with lush chains of petals. The meandering cobblestone path through the scatter garden has fern moss covering the aged gray stones. Sweet little meadowlarks flutter through the blossoms with a joyful song. Wildflower Cottage is a visual delight blooming with perennial flowers in sunlit shades of lavender, poppy, daffodil.

assemblySteps: Trace triangle and square templates on acrylic, including the dots. Cut out acrylic templates and use an ice pick to make a hole at each of the dots. Mark the 3 dots on the back side of each small 3.375 inch triangle. Mark the dots at the corners of each 2 inch square.

Refer to the Triangle Units chart to choose 2 fabrics for each unit. Cut out and mark the 4 triangles needed for a unit. Matching dots, join the 4 triangles to make a pieced unit. Make the units in the numbers shown (A-W: Make 4-12 each of various combinations). Pin together groups of like Triangle Units and label them with letters.
Sew Fabric 23 triangles to each Fabric 8 or Fabric 9 square (120 total units).

Make 49 Sashes: Refer to Sash Piecing diagram and Sashing Blocks chart. Select appropriate units from Step 2 for each sash. Arrange pieced triangles and Fabric 10 (3.5 inch) squares. Join parts into diagonal rows, then join rows to make a Sash.
Make 22 Border Blocks: Arrange a triangle from Step 2, 2 triangles from Step 3, and a Fabric 10 (5.5 inch) quarter-square triangle. Note which blocks use Fabric 8 and which use Fabric 9 patches.
Make 6 horizontal sash rows: Arrange and join 4 Sashes with 5 Fabric 7 (4.75 inch) squares. Press seam allowances toward the squares.
Make 5 block rows: Arrange and join 5 Sashes with 4 floral 9 inch squares. Press seam allowances toward the squares.
Join the rows, alternating sash rows and block rows.
Make 2 side borders: Arrange and join Fabric 7 rectangles with Border Blocks, orienting blocks so gray fabric is on outer edges. Sew to sides of quilt.
Make top and bottom borders: Join Fabric 7 rectangles and squares with Border Blocks. Sew to quilt.
Layer quilt with batting and backing and baste. Quilt in the ditch around all patches. Quilt around printed motifs in squares. Bind to finish.
